description_long: This object consists of a group of box-shaped first-aid kits, each with a weathered wooden exterior and a prominent red cross symbol on the sides, indicating their emergency medical purpose. The boxes have a rustic, slightly worn appearance, with visible wood grain and some surface discoloration, suggesting frequent use or exposure to the elements. The tops of the boxes are open, revealing a dark, empty interior, which implies they are designed to store or transport medical supplies. One of the boxes in the group is plain brown with no visible markings, while the others display the red cross symbol within a faded white circle. The boxes are stacked and arranged in a way that suggests they are portable and can be easily accessed in an emergency. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and visibility in urban or outdoor environments.
description: A group of weathered wooden first-aid kit boxes, most featuring a red cross symbol, with open tops and a utilitarian, rugged appearance.
description_view_0: Three stacked wooden boxes with red cross symbols and one plain brown box, all with open tops and visible wood grain.
description_view_1: Front view showing two red cross boxes stacked behind a plain brown box, highlighting the open tops and emergency markings.
description_view_2: Side view with the plain brown box separated from the group of three red cross boxes, emphasizing the contrast in markings.
description_view_3: Two red cross boxes stacked vertically, both with open tops and weathered wood texture visible.
